Emphasis on security and reliability at scale

enrichment.short_description: CraftMyPDF is a cloud-based API and drag-and-drop template editor for generating PDF documents and images dynamically from data, without requiring code for template design. It supports both synchronous and asynchronous generation at scale, interactive fillable PDF forms, and regional endpoints for data residency. CraftMyPDF is built for developers and no-code teams automating document workflows such as invoices, contracts, and reports.
enrichment.pricing_model: Freemium, tiered monthly or annual subscription priced by number of PDFs/images generated and templates allowed, with annual billing offering a discount
enrichment.starting_price: Free plan with 50 PDFs/images per month; paid plans start at 29 USD per month (Lite plan)
enrichment.seo_title: CraftMyPDF Review 2026: PDF Generation API and Pricing
enrichment.seo_meta_description: CraftMyPDF turns templates into PDFs via API. See 2026 pricing plans, features, integrations, pros, cons, and FAQs for document automation.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: CraftMyPDF is a cloud-based PDF generation API and template design platform founded in 2021 by Jacky Tan and headquartered in Singapore. It was built to let developers and no-code teams turn structured data into polished PDF documents or images at scale, without needing to build and maintain custom PDF rendering infrastructure. The platform is centered on a drag-and-drop template editor that lets users design document layouts visually, placing text fields, images, tables, and other elements onto a canvas, then binding those elements to dynamic data fields that get populated automatically when a document is generated through the API. Templates support rich formatting, including custom fonts, and built-in expressions for formatting dates, currencies, numbers, and text, as well as the ability to extend templates with custom JavaScript functions for more advanced logic. Beyond static documents, CraftMyPDF supports interactive fillable PDF forms, letting designers add components such as text fields, checkboxes, dropdown menus, radio buttons, and digital signature fields directly in the same drag-and-drop editor, which is useful for generating contracts, applications, and other documents that end users need to fill in after generation. The core product is accessed primarily through a REST API, and CraftMyPDF also offers native integrations with Zapier, Make (formerly Integromat), Bubble, and Coda, allowing non-developers to connect PDF generation into existing automation workflows without writing custom API calls. For generation performance and data residency, CraftMyPDF offers regional endpoints in the United States, European Union, Singapore, and Australia, letting customers keep document processing closer to their users or within specific regulatory jurisdictions. On the security and compliance side, CraftMyPDF has completed a SOC 2 Type II audit, which is intended to give business customers confidence in the platform's security, reliability, and operational controls, and it offers a Data Processing Agreement starting from its Lite plan for customers with data protection requirements. Pricing follows a freemium, usage-based tiered model measured primarily in number of PDFs or images generated per month and number of templates allowed. The Free plan includes 50 PDFs or images and 3 templates per month with no credit card required. The Lite plan is 29 USD per month for 1,200 PDFs or images and 6 templates, and includes DPA availability. The Plus plan is 49 USD per month for 3,000 PDFs or images and 15 templates, adding live chat support. The Professional plan is 99 USD per month for 12,000 PDFs or images and 50 templates, and introduces team collaboration, the ability to bring your own cloud storage, and PDF password protection. The Premium plan is 299 USD per month for 40,000 PDFs or images and 1,000 templates with priority support across email, live chat, and video calls. The Business plan is 499 USD per month for 150,000 PDFs or images and 10,000 templates, aimed at large-scale document automation operations. Annual billing is available across paid tiers and can save up to roughly 17 percent compared to monthly billing. CraftMyPDF is typically compared to competitors such as PDF Generator API, PDFMonkey, and APITemplate.io, and is generally chosen by SaaS companies, agencies, and internal engineering teams that need to generate invoices, contracts, reports, certificates, or other data-driven documents automatically as part of a larger product or workflow.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is CraftMyPDF
paragraphs: CraftMyPDF is a template-based PDF and image generation platform accessed through a REST API, paired with a visual drag-and-drop editor for designing document layouts without writing rendering code.

Templates are bound to dynamic data fields so that a single design can be reused to generate large numbers of personalized documents, such as invoices or certificates, each populated with different data through an API call.

long_form_blocks.heading: Fillable Forms and No-Code Integrations
paragraphs: Beyond static output, CraftMyPDF supports interactive fillable PDF forms with text fields, checkboxes, dropdowns, radio buttons, and signature fields designed directly in the drag-and-drop editor.
Native integrations with Zapier, Make, Bubble, and Coda let no-code teams incorporate PDF generation into broader automation workflows without writing custom API integration code.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing Tiers and Security
paragraphs: CraftMyPDF pricing scales with monthly PDF or image generation volume and number of templates, from a free 50-document tier up to a 499 USD per month Business plan supporting 150,000 documents.
The platform has completed a SOC 2 Type II audit and offers a Data Processing Agreement starting from the Lite plan, supporting customers with formal security and data protection requirements.

features.title: Drag-and-drop template editor
features.description: Design PDF and image templates visually by placing text, images, and tables onto a canvas without writing rendering code.

features.title: Dynamic data binding
features.description: Bind template elements to data fields so a single template can generate many personalized documents through the API.

features.title: Interactive fillable PDF forms
features.description: Add fillable text fields, checkboxes, dropdowns, radio buttons, and signature fields directly in the template editor.

features.title: Built-in formatting expressions
features.description: Format dates, currencies, numbers, and text with built-in expressions, or extend templates with custom JavaScript.

features.title: Synchronous and asynchronous generation
features.description: Supports both real-time PDF generation and asynchronous batch generation for scale.

features.title: Regional data residency endpoints
features.description: Offers processing endpoints in the US, EU, Singapore, and Australia for latency and data residency needs.

features.title: No-code automation integrations
features.description: Native integrations with Zapier, Make, Bubble, and Coda connect PDF generation to existing workflows without custom code.

features.title: SOC 2 Type II compliance
features.description: Has completed a SOC 2 Type II audit and offers a Data Processing Agreement for customers with compliance requirements.

pros: No-code template editor lowers the barrier to designing data-driven PDF layouts
Supports interactive fillable forms, not just static documents
Regional endpoints support data residency and lower latency for global users
SOC 2 Type II compliance and DPA availability suit business and compliance-conscious customers
Free plan and relatively affordable entry-tier pricing compared to some competitors
cons: Monthly document limits require plan upgrades as usage scales, similar to other usage-based PDF APIs
Team collaboration features are not available until the Professional plan
Advanced priority support (live chat, Zoom) is reserved for the Premium and Business tiers
As a small, relatively young company (founded 2021), long-term enterprise support depth is less established than larger incumbents
Complex, highly customized layouts may still require JavaScript expressions for advanced formatting logic

faqs.question: What is CraftMyPDF used for
faqs.answer: CraftMyPDF is used to generate PDF documents and images dynamically from data, using reusable templates designed in a drag-and-drop editor and populated through a REST API.

faqs.question: Does CraftMyPDF have a free plan
faqs.answer: Yes, CraftMyPDF offers a free plan with 50 PDFs or images and 3 templates per month, with no credit card required.

faqs.question: How much does CraftMyPDF cost
faqs.answer: Paid plans start at 29 USD per month for the Lite plan (1,200 PDFs/images, 6 templates) and scale up to 499 USD per month for the Business plan (150,000 PDFs/images, 10,000 templates), with annual billing offering savings.

faqs.question: Can CraftMyPDF create fillable PDF forms
faqs.answer: Yes, CraftMyPDF supports interactive fillable PDF forms with text fields, checkboxes, dropdowns, radio buttons, and signature fields designed in its editor.

faqs.question: Does CraftMyPDF integrate with Zapier or Make
faqs.answer: Yes, CraftMyPDF offers native integrations with Zapier, Make, Bubble, and Coda, in addition to its REST API.

faqs.question: Is CraftMyPDF SOC 2 compliant
faqs.answer: Yes, CraftMyPDF has completed a SOC 2 Type II audit and offers a Data Processing Agreement starting from its Lite plan.

faqs.question: Does CraftMyPDF support data residency
faqs.answer: Yes, CraftMyPDF offers regional processing endpoints in the United States, European Union, Singapore, and Australia.

faqs.question: Who founded CraftMyPDF
faqs.answer: CraftMyPDF was founded in 2021 by Jacky Tan and is headquartered in Singapore.

use_cases.title: Automated invoice generation
use_cases.description: SaaS platforms use CraftMyPDF to generate branded invoices automatically from billing data whenever a payment or order is processed.

use_cases.title: Contract and agreement generation with signatures
use_cases.description: Businesses generate contracts with fillable text fields and signature fields using CraftMyPDF's interactive form support.
use_cases.audience: Legal, HR, and sales operations teams

use_cases.title: No-code document automation
use_cases.description: Teams using Zapier, Make, or Bubble connect CraftMyPDF to their existing workflows to generate reports or certificates without writing API integration code.

use_cases.title: Large-scale personalized document generation
use_cases.description: Organizations use asynchronous batch generation to produce large volumes of personalized documents, such as certificates or statements, at scale.
use_cases.audience: Enterprises and education or membership organizations

features: Priority support via email, live chat, and video calls
All Premium features for large-scale operations

enrichment.short_description: CrateDB is a distributed SQL database designed for real-time analytics on large volumes of structured, semi-structured, and time-series data, combining relational SQL querying with document and full-text search capabilities. Originally created in 2013 in Austria, the database is developed by Crate.io, now headquartered in Redwood City, California, with additional offices in Berlin and Austria. CrateDB is available as an open-source self-managed edition, a licensed self-managed Enterprise edition, and a fully managed CrateDB Cloud service.
enrichment.pricing_model: Open-source community edition available for free; usage-based pay-as-you-go pricing for CrateDB Cloud based on compute and storage; custom licensing and support pricing for self-managed Enterprise edition
enrichment.starting_price: Free open-source Community edition and a free CrateDB Cloud tier for evaluation; paid CrateDB Cloud usage-based pricing starts at roughly 53 USD per month depending on resources
enrichment.headquarters: Redwood City, California, United States (with offices in Berlin, Germany and Dornbirn, Austria)
enrichment.seo_title: CrateDB Review 2026: Real-Time SQL Database Pricing
enrichment.seo_meta_description: CrateDB is a distributed SQL database for real-time analytics. Compare Cloud and Enterprise pricing, features, pros, cons, and FAQs for 2026.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: CrateDB is a distributed SQL database built for real-time analytics on large volumes of data, originally started in 2013 in Dornbirn, Austria, by Christian Lutz, Bernd Dorn, and Jodok Batlogg as an open-source, clustered database purpose-built for fast text search and analytics. The company behind CrateDB, Crate.io, raised its first round of financing in 2014 and has since grown into a distributed team, with its current headquarters at 541 Jefferson Avenue, Redwood City, California, United States, and additional offices in Berlin, Germany, and in Dornbirn and Vienna, Austria, reflecting the product's European origins alongside its US commercial headquarters. CrateDB is written in Java and built on a shared-nothing distributed architecture designed for horizontal scalability, allowing it to handle high-volume ingestion and querying workloads common in Internet of Things (IoT), industrial, and real-time monitoring use cases. Architecturally, CrateDB combines a standard SQL query interface with the flexibility of a document-oriented and full-text-searchable data store, letting developers query structured relational data, semi-structured JSON-like documents, and time-series data using familiar SQL syntax rather than needing separate systems for search, analytics, and transactional queries. This hybrid relational and NoSQL approach is a key differentiator versus purely relational databases like PostgreSQL or purely document-oriented databases like MongoDB, positioning CrateDB specifically for real-time analytics over large, fast-moving datasets rather than traditional OLTP transaction processing. CrateDB is distributed in three main forms. The Community edition is free and open source, allowing organizations to self-host and manage their own CrateDB clusters without licensing cost, though without vendor-provided production tooling or support. The self-managed Enterprise edition adds licensed production tooling, including automation, observability integrations, and access to professional services such as Go-Live assistance, performance tuning, legacy system migration, cloud migration support, and Resident Engineer programs, aimed at organizations that want to run CrateDB on their own infrastructure but need vendor support and operational tooling to do so reliably at scale. CrateDB Cloud is the fully managed service offering, letting customers deploy, monitor, back up, and scale CrateDB clusters on AWS, Microsoft Azure, or Google Cloud without managing the database software themselves; it uses a usage-based, pay-as-you-go pricing model billed hourly or monthly based on compute and storage consumption, with volume discounts available, and includes a free tier for evaluation and prototyping. Published pricing editions for CrateDB Cloud have ranged from roughly 53 USD per month at the low end up to over 1,400 USD per month for larger dedicated clusters, scaling with resource allocation. Key CrateDB Cloud features include an SQL Scheduler for automating recurring queries and maintenance tasks, table policies for managing data lifecycle, an embedded browser-based query console for exploring data directly, built-in data connectors for importing and streaming data in real time, and automatic backups for data protection and recovery. CrateDB is commonly used for IoT sensor data platforms, industrial monitoring and predictive maintenance systems, log and event analytics, and other applications that require ingesting and querying very large volumes of time-stamped or semi-structured data in near real time using standard SQL tooling.

long_form_blocks.heading: What Is CrateDB
paragraphs: CrateDB is a distributed SQL database that combines relational query capabilities with document storage and full-text search, built on a shared-nothing architecture for horizontal scalability across large, fast-moving datasets.

It was originally created in 2013 in Austria as an open-source clustered database for fast search and analytics, and is now developed by Crate.io, headquartered in Redwood City, California, with offices in Berlin and Austria.

long_form_blocks.heading: Community, Enterprise, and Cloud Editions
paragraphs: CrateDB is available as a free, open-source Community edition for self-hosting, a licensed self-managed Enterprise edition that adds production tooling and professional services, and CrateDB Cloud, a fully managed service on AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud.
CrateDB Cloud uses usage-based, pay-as-you-go pricing billed hourly or monthly by compute and storage, with a free tier available for evaluation and volume discounts for larger deployments.

long_form_blocks.heading: Real-Time Analytics and IoT Workloads
paragraphs: CrateDB's architecture is optimized for ingesting and querying large volumes of time-series and semi-structured data using standard SQL, making it a common choice for IoT sensor platforms, industrial monitoring, and log or event analytics.

Because it supports SQL alongside document and full-text search capabilities, teams can avoid maintaining separate systems for structured queries, semi-structured data, and search within the same application.

features.title: Distributed SQL querying
features.description: Supports standard SQL syntax over a horizontally scalable, shared-nothing distributed architecture.

features.title: Hybrid relational and document storage
features.description: Combines relational tables with document-oriented and full-text-searchable data storage in one system.

features.title: Real-time analytics at scale
features.description: Optimized for ingesting and querying large volumes of time-series and semi-structured data with low latency.

features.title: Fully managed CrateDB Cloud
features.description: Deploys, monitors, backs up, and scales clusters on AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud without customer-managed database operations.

features.title: SQL Scheduler and table policies
features.description: Automates recurring queries, maintenance tasks, and data lifecycle management within CrateDB Cloud.

features.title: Built-in data connectors
features.description: Supports importing and streaming data into CrateDB in real time through built-in connectors.

features.title: Embedded query console
features.description: Provides a browser-based console for exploring and querying data directly without external tooling.

features.title: Automatic backups
features.description: CrateDB Cloud includes automatic backups for data protection and fast recovery.

pros: Combines SQL, document storage, and full-text search in a single system, reducing the need for separate tools
Horizontally scalable, shared-nothing architecture suited to high-volume IoT and time-series workloads
Open-source Community edition available at no cost for self-hosting
Fully managed Cloud option removes operational overhead for teams that do not want to run their own clusters
Usage-based Cloud pricing with a free evaluation tier lowers the barrier to trying the product
cons: Smaller ecosystem and community compared to mainstream databases like PostgreSQL or MySQL
Enterprise self-managed edition and professional services pricing is custom and not publicly listed
Usage-based Cloud pricing can be harder to predict for highly variable workloads than flat-rate plans
As a specialized real-time analytics database, it is not designed as a general-purpose OLTP transactional database
Relatively small company (51-200 employees) compared to larger database vendors, which may affect long-term roadmap pace

faqs.question: What is CrateDB used for
faqs.answer: CrateDB is used for real-time analytics on large volumes of data, commonly in IoT, industrial monitoring, and log or event analytics use cases, using standard SQL over a distributed architecture.

faqs.question: Is CrateDB open source
faqs.answer: Yes, CrateDB has a free, open-source Community edition for self-hosting, in addition to a licensed Enterprise edition and a fully managed CrateDB Cloud service.

faqs.question: How much does CrateDB Cloud cost
faqs.answer: CrateDB Cloud uses usage-based, pay-as-you-go pricing based on compute and storage, with published editions ranging from roughly 53 USD per month up to over 1,400 USD per month for larger dedicated clusters, plus a free evaluation tier.

faqs.question: Who develops CrateDB
faqs.answer: CrateDB is developed by Crate.io, a company originally started in 2013 in Dornbirn, Austria, now headquartered in Redwood City, California, with offices in Berlin and Austria.

faqs.question: Can CrateDB run on AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud
faqs.answer: Yes, CrateDB Cloud can be deployed on AWS, Microsoft Azure, or Google Cloud as a fully managed service.

faqs.question: Does CrateDB support full-text search
faqs.answer: Yes, CrateDB combines relational SQL querying with document-oriented storage and full-text search capabilities in a single system.

faqs.question: Is CrateDB good for IoT data
faqs.answer: Yes, CrateDB's distributed, horizontally scalable architecture is commonly used for ingesting and querying large volumes of IoT sensor and time-series data in real time.

faqs.question: What is the difference between CrateDB Community and Enterprise editions
faqs.answer: The Community edition is free and open source for self-hosting without vendor tooling, while the Enterprise edition is a licensed self-managed offering that adds production automation, observability tooling, and professional services support.

use_cases.title: IoT sensor data platforms
use_cases.description: Organizations use CrateDB to ingest and query high-volume sensor data from IoT devices in near real time using standard SQL.

use_cases.title: Industrial monitoring and predictive maintenance
use_cases.description: Manufacturers use CrateDB to analyze equipment telemetry data in real time to detect anomalies and predict maintenance needs.

use_cases.title: Log and event analytics
use_cases.description: Engineering teams use CrateDB to store and query large volumes of log and event data for monitoring and troubleshooting.

use_cases.title: Real-time dashboards on large datasets
use_cases.description: Analytics teams build real-time dashboards on top of CrateDB's SQL interface to visualize large, fast-changing datasets.

features: Standard SQL, document, and full-text search capabilities
Deploy on AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud
